overview.title: System Overview
overview.summary: The complete application at the service-role level. Click any major application node to drill into the function graph for that area.
overview.hint: Click: Startup, Webhooks, Queue, Jobs, Teams Bot, Session Memory, Agent + MCP, MCP Tools, Card Actions, Integrations, State, or Observability.
explanation: The hosted deployment is one Azure Container App process plus a FastMCP subprocess. Webhooks and Teams share the same Python server.
Webhook work is accepted quickly and handed to a queue. Teams natural language goes through the agent and MCP tools. Card buttons and webhook jobs use deterministic Python flows.
Durable state lives in Cosmos DB or local JSON, jobs live in Azure Storage Queue, and external business operations go through Microsoft Graph, DocuSign, Teams, Outlook, and optional LLM providers.

webhooks.title: Webhook Ingress Function Graph
webhooks.summary: Request-time parsing and durable handoff. Webhook handlers enqueue jobs and return quickly.
webhooks.hint: Webhook handlers do not perform expensive tracker, DocuSign, or email work inline.
explanation: Power Automate webhooks validate `X-Webhook-Secret`, parse JSON, and enqueue a queue job.
DocuSign webhooks parse XML or JSON envelope status data and enqueue a DocuSign job.
The accepted HTTP response only means the job was accepted by the queue path.

queue.title: Queue Function Graph
queue.summary: Local and Azure queue backends convert webhook jobs into calls to `process_job()`.
queue.hint: Azure queue messages are deleted only after successful processing.
explanation: `create_job_queue()` chooses local in-process tasks for development or Azure Storage Queue for production.
The Azure worker polls one message at a time, deserializes the JSON job, calls `process_job()`, and deletes the message on success.
Malformed or over-dequeued poison messages are deleted.

worker["AzureStorageJobQueue._run_worker()"]

handle["_handle_message(message)"]
process["process_job(job)"]
storage["Azure Storage Queue"]

hrJob.title: HR Submission Job Detail
hrJob.summary: Detailed deterministic flow for Power Automate HR submissions.
hrJob.hint: The workflow type controls which handler runs and which Teams card actions are exposed.
explanation: `process_new_hire_job()` is the queue consumer for all HR submission workflows, not just literal new hires.
It normalizes `statusChange`, routes to the right workflow handler, ensures the tracker row exists, applies policy stage updates, then posts or updates the Teams workflow card.
The same common field extraction and composite identity checks are used across new-hire, onboarding, offboarding, and temporary-status workflows.

memory.title: Session Memory Function Graph
memory.summary: Conversation history and compact session context connect proactive card threads to later natural-language replies.
memory.hint: This is backed by `store_mod.session_store`, which is Cosmos in production or files locally.
explanation: Session keys differ for DMs, channels, and channel threads.
Proactive card posts seed thread context so replies in that thread inherit employee and submission identity.
Chat history is bounded; structured session context carries the high-value fields.
